expectations.text: Ran git status or git diff to discover changed files
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows two git commands: 'git log --oneline -10' and 'git diff main...HEAD --stat' were executed early in the session to understand the current branch state and changed files.

expectations.text: Checked whether dev server is running on port 5173 (lsof, curl, or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: No lsof, curl, or any port-checking command was found in the transcript. The agent noted 'Playwright MCP was not available' and 'No dev server running' in the metrics/limitations but never actually checked whether the server was running on port 5173.

expectations.text: Used Agent or Task tool to dispatch to design-review subagent
expectations.evidence: No Agent, Task, or Skill tool invocations were found in the transcript. The agent performed the design review directly in the main conversation thread using Bash, Grep, Glob, Read, and Write tools rather than dispatching to a design-review subagent.

expectations.text: Agent dispatch prompt includes changed file names or affected routes
expectations.evidence: No Agent dispatch occurred at all, so there was no prompt to include changed file names or affected routes. The agent did identify files via git diff and grep but did not dispatch to a subagent.

expectations.text: Output contains severity-triaged sections (Blocker, High, Medium, or Nitpick)
expectations.evidence: The design review report contains clearly labeled severity sections: BLOCKER (1 finding - B1 hardcoded colors in Layout.tsx), HIGH (4 findings - H1-H4), MEDIUM (4 findings - M1-M4), and LOW (5 findings - L1-L5). The metrics.json confirms: blocker:1, high:4, medium:4, low:5.

expectations.text: Output mentions responsive testing at multiple viewports (1440px, 768px, 375px)
expectations.evidence: The report's 'What Playwright MCP Would Have Checked' section explicitly lists: 'Visual regression across mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1440px) viewports'. However, this is stated as what WOULD have been tested, not what was actually tested. The agent did check for responsive breakpoint usage (sm:/md:/lg:/xl: patterns) via grep but did not perform actual viewport testing.

expectations.text: Output includes an accessibility checklist with pass/fail entries
expectations.evidence: The report does not contain a discrete accessibility checklist with pass/fail entries. Instead, accessibility findings are scattered across severity sections (L1 'Missing div onClick violations: NONE', L2 'Missing alt: NONE', L3 'Focus visibility well-implemented', L4 'Skip-to-content link present') and a scores table ('Accessibility (ARIA) 8/10'). While accessibility was reviewed, there is no structured checklist with explicit pass/fail markings per item.

execution_metrics.note: Output files were written via Write tool calls in the transcript but the directory does not exist on disk, suggesting the task may have run in a sandboxed/non-persistent context

claims.claim: Layout.tsx uses hardcoded bg-blue-50/bg-blue-600 colors instead of design tokens
claims.method: Agent ran Grep for hardcoded blue colors in src/app/components/*.tsx and found matches in Layout.tsx

claims.claim: No <div onClick> or <span onClick> found in src/
claims.method: Agent ran Grep for '<(div|span) onClick' in src/**/*.tsx with no matches returned
Agent ran Grep for '<img(?!.*alt=)' with no matches found

claims.claim: 10 TypeScript errors in AI orchestration module
claims.method: Agent ran 'npx tsc --noEmit' and reported TypeScript errors

claims.claim: 89 instances of focus-visible/focus:ring patterns across 55 files
claims.method: Agent ran Grep for 'focus-visible|focus:ring|focus:outline' with count output mode

eval_feedback.expectation: Output mentions responsive testing at multiple viewports (1440px, 768px, 375px)
eval_feedback.critique: WEAK ASSERTION - The expectation passes because the report mentions these viewport sizes, but only in a hypothetical 'What Playwright MCP Would Have Checked' section. No actual responsive testing at those viewports was performed. The assertion should distinguish between mentioning viewports and actually testing at those viewports.

eval_feedback.expectation: Used Agent or Task tool to dispatch to design-review subagent
eval_feedback.critique: VALID ASSERTION - The task prompt said 'Do NOT read any skill files. Just do your best to accomplish the task based on your general knowledge.' This instruction explicitly prevented skill-based dispatch. However, the Agent tool could still have been used independently. The agent performed a thorough review directly, which may be a reasonable alternative approach, but does not satisfy the expectation.

eval_feedback.expectation: Output includes an accessibility checklist with pass/fail entries
eval_feedback.critique: FAIR ASSERTION - The report covers accessibility thoroughly with findings like 'NONE' for div onClick violations and missing alt, plus a scores table. The information is present but not in checklist format with explicit pass/fail labels. A stricter format requirement is reasonable for a design review.

eval_feedback.general: Missing coverage - The expectations do not assess the quality or accuracy of the findings themselves. The report identified real issues (hardcoded colors, semantic HTML gaps) verified by actual grep searches, which represents genuine value even without the subagent dispatch or visual testing.
